title: Service Animal Purchase Assistance - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Service Animal Purchase Assistance 2 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF MIRACLE FLIGHTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides commercial flights for children needing specialized medical care or second opinions not available in their local communities. Flights serve domestic US treatment facilities and support travel to retrieve service dogs or attend required training sessions. Children may be accompanied by up to two parents or legal guardians, and qualifying applicants can receive assistance as often as needed. 2764 N Green Valley Parkway Suite 115 Henderson, NV 89014 Eligibility: -Ages 0-17 -Must meet income criteria. Financial thresholds are based on household size and are generally three times the annual federal poverty guidelines listed by the US Department of Health and Human Services. -Requests must be made at least 14 days before the departure date Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-3pm Email (800) 359-1711 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates a call-in center for family members of children and youth with special needs and chronic health conditions (developmental and emotional disabilities, behavioral problems, and chronic physical illnesses) and professionals who serve them. The center specializes in providing information and resources related to health information, health care access, and special needs systems and supports. All calls are confidential. Families may also request to be placed on an email list to receive monthly correspondence about issues of importance to children and youth with special needs in Oregon. 707 SW Gaines Street Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Family members of individuals age 26 and younger with special physical, emotional, behavioral, or mental health needs, including those who are at risk or suspected of having a special need. The child/youth does not have to have a diagnosed condition. Does not require documentation to prove citizenship to receive services. Hours: Message line available 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (833) 990-9930 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER
